188F:01A0.label: func_run_dialog
188F:01A0.comment:
Sets up and shows the dialog at struct 0x03B0, then handles input.
Populates status bar text (0x43DC) and title string (0x40F6) before drawing.
Called in response to a key press (F2). Closes and restores screen on exit.

188F:0E95.label: func_note_sequencer
188F:0E95.comment:
Music note sequencer called each timer tick from func_int8_music_tick.
Decrements tick counter [0x125D]; returns early if not yet expired.
On expiry: advances voice state, reloads counter with 0x17 (23 ticks), then updates
the on-screen note display by writing char/attr words to the CGA video buffer (ES=0xB800)
with retrace sync (port [0x02FC]=0x03DA). Calls func_0AF0 on note transitions.
Dispatches to the next note-processing step via call si ([0x1266]).
Does NOT write to the OPL chip directly — FM output is handled by func_fm_voice_update.

188F:11E0.label: func_set_pit_rate
188F:11E0.comment:
Programs the PIT timer with a rate from the frequency table at 0x171E.
Index is [0x176C]; reload value stored in [0x17A0] then written to PIT via func_program_pit.

188F:11F3.label: func_load_sound_config
188F:11F3.comment:
Initializes FM voice state and loads sound parameters from config block at 0x43B0.
Iterates 5 voice structs at 0x1586, then reads settings (volume, tempo, voices, SB params)
from 0x43B0 via lodsw and distributes them to their respective state variables.

188F:12E1.label: func_start_audio
188F:12E1.comment:
Starts the audio system: loads sound config, programs the PIT timer rate,
saves current INT 8/9/SB vectors, then installs own INT 8 (music tick at 188F:132E)
and INT 9 (keyboard at 188F:1381) handlers. Sets [0x17AA]=1 when done.

188F:151A.label: func_program_pit
188F:151A.comment:
Programs PIT channel 0 with the reload value in AX.
Writes mode byte 0x36 (ch0, mode 3, 16-bit binary) to port 0x43, then AX low/high to port 0x40.

188F:1960.label: func_fm_voice_update
188F:1960.comment:
Iterates all 5 FM voice structs at 0x1586 (stride 0x1D = 29 bytes each, end = 0x1617).
For each active voice (byte [bx+0x1A] != 0), decrements the per-voice timer at [bx+0x10].
When the timer expires (goes negative), calls the OPL write callback via call [bx]
(function pointer stored at voice struct offset 0). This is where FM register writes
actually reach the sound card.

188F:17C0.label: func_save_int_vectors
188F:17C0.comment:
Saves current INT 8 (timer), INT 9 (keyboard), and SB hardware IRQ vectors.
INT number for SB IRQ is read from [0x38B0]. Saved to [0x1770..177A] for later restore.

188F:57F0.label: func_dsp_write
188F:57F0.comment:
Write one byte to the DSP command/data port (base+0xC = 0x22C).
Polls the write-buffer status by reading port 0x22C: loops while bit 7 is set (busy).
Writes AL to port 0x22C once the buffer is free.
[0x38A6] = SB base port (0x0220).

188F:5806.label: func_dsp_reset
188F:5806.comment:
Resets the SB DSP: writes 0x01 then 0x00 to port 0x226 (base+6) with a 256-iteration delay.
Polls port 0x22E (status) for bit 7 set, then reads 0x22A expecting 0xAA (ready byte).
Returns AX=0 on success.

188F:7F5A.label: func_sb_dma_detect
188F:7F5A.comment:
SB DMA identification probe. Arg: [bp+8] = parameter byte (0xA5 in caller).
Installs a temporary IRQ5 handler at 188F:7FBF, programs DMA channel 1 for a
1-byte WRITE (device→memory) transfer to the stack slot [bp+8], then sends
DSP command 0xE2 + param to trigger the SB's DMA identification transfer.
Spins up to 0x600 iterations waiting for DMA to write a non-zero result byte.
Returns AX = DMA result byte, or 0 on timeout.
Caller (func_sb_setup) checks for 0xDD: 0xDD = param (0xA5) XOR 0x78,
confirming the SB responded correctly. If result ≠ 0xDD, func_dialog_event_loop
is patched to a single ret.

188F:069A.label: func_sb_setup
188F:069A.comment:
Sound Blaster setup and card-selection wizard.
Stores a far pointer to func_dialog_event_loop at [bp-8] for conditional patching.
Calls func_188F_3C00 (SB hardware detection) and func_188F_5680; if either returns 0
(no card found) various init steps are skipped.
Shows the SB card-selection dialog via func_188F_7F5A (arg=0x00A5); if the result is
not 0xDD (user cancelled or no card configured), writes 0xC3 (ret) directly into the
first byte of func_dialog_event_loop, permanently disabling it for this session.

188F:2BBA.label: func_dialog_event_loop
188F:2BBA.comment:
Full SB-backed dialog event loop — but self-patched to a single ret when SB is absent.
func_sb_setup (188F:069A) stores this address as a far pointer at [bp-8] and, if the
SB card-selection dialog does not return 0xDD (success), executes:
  les bx, [bp-8]          ; ES:BX = 188F:2BBA
  mov byte es:[bx], 0xC3  ; overwrite entry point with ret
This happens once at startup; all subsequent calls to func_dialog_event_loop return
immediately without entering the keyboard/event loop.

188F:4FEF.label: func_save_screen_region
188F:4FEF.comment:
Saves a rectangular text-mode screen region to a memory buffer.
Args: start_pos (AH=row, AL=col), end_pos (AH=row, AL=col), dest (far ptr), attr_filter
Synchronizes each word copy with the CGA retrace signal to avoid snow.

188F:50EA.label: func_draw_dialog_box
188F:50EA.comment:
Draws a dialog box from a struct ptr (bp+0x04).
Struct: +00 top_left, +02 bot_right (AH=row/AL=col), +04..09 border chars (┌─┐│└┘),
+0a title str ptr, +0c/0e screen save buf (allocated here), +10 title attr, +11 body attr,
+12 item attr, +18 items array ptr, +1a status bar str ptr (0=skip status bar save).
Returns 1 on success.

188F:506C.label: func_restore_screen_region
188F:506C.comment:
Restores a rectangular screen region from a memory buffer back to the CGA video buffer.
Mirror of func_save_screen_region: reads from source far ptr, writes to B800 with retrace sync.

188F:55DA.label: func_close_dialog_box
188F:55DA.comment:
Restores the screen saved by func_draw_dialog_box and frees the buffer.
If struct[+1a] is non-null, also restores the status bar row.
Pair of func_draw_dialog_box.

188F:132E.label: func_int8_music_tick
188F:132E.comment:
INT 8 (timer) handler installed by func_start_audio. Fires at the custom PIT tick rate.
Accumulates PIT reload value [0x17A0] into [0x179E]; on carry (overflow), chains to the
original BIOS INT 8 vector at [0x1774] via pushf+call (software-interrupt simulation)
to keep the 18.2 Hz system clock ticking. Without carry, sends EOI directly to PIC1.
Re-entrant guard [0x17A6] prevents nested invocations.
On each tick: increments position counter [0x17A2], then calls func_0E95 (note engine)
and func_1960 (FM register update).

188F:1381.label: func_int9_keyboard
188F:1381.comment:
INT 9 (keyboard) handler installed by func_start_audio.
Reads scan code from port 0x60, saves to [0x17A7], acknowledges hardware via port 0x61
(pulse bit 7), dispatches to the handler at [0x17B7] (= func_kbd_dispatcher), then sends EOI.
Does NOT chain to the original INT 9 handler — all keyboard input is captured until
func_stop_audio restores the original vector. This is the root cause of the apparent
lockup after the dialog closes with the stub event loop.

188F:1495.label: func_kbd_dispatcher
188F:1495.comment:
Keyboard scan code dispatcher called indirectly from func_int9_keyboard via [0x17B7].
Writes the current scan code ([0x17A7]) into the scan code buffer at far ptr [0x17A2:17A4],
advancing the position if the current slot is already occupied.
Then calls func_10D2 to check whether the key matches a recognized dialog action;
if so (ZF set), dispatches to the handler in BX.

lineComments.188F:01CC: 0x43DC = status bar text buffer for this dialog (struct[+1a]); populated before drawing
lineComments.188F:01FA: 0x40F6 = dialog title string buffer (struct[+0a]); populated before drawing
lineComments.188F:020E: func_draw_dialog_box returns 0 if buffer allocation failed; skip input loop in that case
lineComments.188F:021C: close dialog: restore screen from saved buffer and free it
lineComments.188F:0E97: [0x125D] = tick countdown; fires note event when it goes negative (every ~23 ticks)
lineComments.188F:0EB2: reload tick counter with 0x17 (23 ticks) for the next note interval
lineComments.188F:0F46: ES = [0x02FA] = 0xB800: writing note display to CGA screen buffer, NOT OPL output
lineComments.188F:0F5B: DX = [0x02FC] = 0x03DA: CGA status port for retrace-sync writes
lineComments.188F:0F66: CGA retrace wait (1508 executions per note event); HOT because each note update syncs to display
lineComments.188F:0F91: indirect dispatch via [0x1266] = 0x0F94: configurable pointer to next note-processing step
lineComments.188F:11E8: PIT reload value from frequency table: base 0x171E + [0x176C]*2; controls music tick rate
lineComments.188F:12E8: [0x02FE] = mode flag; 0 = initial state, skip conditional SB hardware init
lineComments.188F:12FF: INT 21h/25h: install own INT 9 (keyboard) handler at 188F:1381
lineComments.188F:1307: INT 21h/25h: install own INT 8 (timer) handler at 188F:132E for music ticks
lineComments.188F:1341: accumulate PIT reload value [0x17A0] into [0x179E]; carry on overflow means BIOS chain is due
lineComments.188F:1345: CF=0 (no overflow): skip BIOS chain, send EOI directly at int8_send_eoi
lineComments.188F:1347: carry set: chain to original BIOS INT 8 via pushf+call far (software-interrupt simulation)
lineComments.188F:134E: no BIOS chain this tick: send EOI to PIC1 directly
lineComments.188F:1352: [0x17A6] = re-entrant guard; 1 = music tick already in progress, skip to epilogue
lineComments.188F:1359: [0x17A6] = 1: mark music tick in progress; cleared at end to allow next tick
lineComments.188F:1360: [0x17A2] = song position counter; 0xFFFF = no active song (skip increment, still tick FM)
lineComments.188F:1390: read keyboard scan code from port 0x60; save to [0x17A7] for func_kbd_dispatcher
lineComments.188F:1395: pulse bit 7 of port 0x61 high then low to acknowledge keyboard to the 8255/8042 controller
lineComments.188F:13A1: [0x17B7] = active keyboard handler (set to func_kbd_dispatcher by func_start_audio)
lineComments.188F:149C: ES:DI = far pointer into scan code buffer: DI=[0x17A2] offset, ES=[0x17A4] segment
lineComments.188F:14A0: if current buffer slot is non-zero (occupied), advance position before writing
lineComments.188F:14AE: write scan code into buffer slot ES:[DI]
lineComments.188F:14B6: func_10D2: check if scan code matches a recognized dialog key; sets ZF and BX if matched
lineComments.188F:14BC: recognized dialog key: call action handler in BX
lineComments.188F:151D: PIT mode byte 0x36: channel 0, mode 3 (square wave), 16-bit binary count
lineComments.188F:1967: [bx+0x1A] = voice active flag; 0 = inactive, skip this voice
lineComments.188F:196D: [bx+0x10] = per-voice timer countdown; fires callback when it goes negative
lineComments.188F:1972: call [bx] = OPL write callback at voice struct offset 0 — actual FM register output to sound card
lineComments.188F:17C0: INT 21h/35h: get INT 9 (keyboard) vector; save offset to [0x1770], segment to [0x1772]
lineComments.188F:17CD: INT 21h/35h: get INT 8 (timer) vector; save to [0x1774/1776]
lineComments.188F:17DA: get SB hardware IRQ vector; interrupt number is dynamic, stored at [0x38B0]
lineComments.188F:4FF9: start_pos: AH=row, AL=col; sequence below computes SI = (row*80 + col)*2, the byte offset in the video buffer
lineComments.188F:5018: 0xB800 = CGA color text segment; mode 7 (MDA mono) would use 0xB000 instead
lineComments.188F:5028: attr_filter: 0 = copy all chars; nonzero enables attribute-filtered path (gap at 502F, never executed)
lineComments.188F:5048: BL=0x09: CGA status mask — bit 0 = display enable / horizontal retrace, bit 3 = vertical sync
lineComments.188F:505B: 0xA0 = 160 bytes per text row (80 cols * 2 bytes each); step to next source row
lineComments.188F:5118: 0xA0 = 160 extra bytes at start of buffer to hold the optional status bar row
lineComments.188F:5255: AH = inner_row_count; packed into AH:00 then added to top_left pos to get first inner-row address
lineComments.188F:52BC: mask bot_right to row-only (clear AL), then add start_col to form bottom-left corner position
lineComments.188F:534B: scan for NUL to measure title string length; not+dec cx converts repne result to strlen
lineComments.188F:5354: halve the unused column gap to center the title: left_offset = (inner_width - title_len) / 2
lineComments.188F:5381: 0x0101: skip one cell in row (AH) and col (AL) inward from each border to get fill area bounds
lineComments.188F:06A8: save func_dialog_event_loop offset (0x2BBA) to [bp-8]; will be used as patch target if SB not configured
lineComments.188F:06AD: save segment (0x188F) to [bp-6]; together with [bp-8] forms far ptr 188F:2BBA for LES patching
lineComments.188F:07DA: SB card-selection dialog result: 0xDD = success/configured; anything else means no SB
lineComments.188F:07DD: if result != 0xDD (no SB configured), jump to self-modify code that disables func_dialog_event_loop
lineComments.188F:07E2: les bx,[bp-8]: reload far ptr 188F:2BBA (func_dialog_event_loop) into ES:BX
lineComments.188F:07E5: self-modify: ES: override + mov byte [bx],0xC3 — writes ret into func_dialog_event_loop entry point
lineComments.188F:7F64: func_188F_59D5 saves current IRQ5 vector so it can be restored after the probe
lineComments.188F:7F76: func_188F_59C0: install 188F:7FBF as IRQ5 (INT 0x0D) handler for the duration of the DMA probe
lineComments.188F:7F80: func_188F_5788: program DMA channel 1 destination to the physical address of [bp+8] (the result slot)
lineComments.188F:7F87: func_188F_5A3A: configure DMA channel 1 — CX=0 (1 byte), DH=0x45 (single-cycle WRITE device→mem, ch1)
lineComments.188F:7F8D: write DSP command 0xE2 (DMA identification): triggers a 1-byte DMA transfer to the programmed destination
lineComments.188F:7F94: xchg: AL gets the probe parameter (0xA5); [bp+8] cleared to 0 — DMA will overwrite it with result
lineComments.188F:7F97: write DSP parameter 0xA5 to complete the 0xE2 command; expected DMA response = 0xA5 XOR 0x78 = 0xDD
lineComments.188F:7F9D: spin loop 0x600 (1536) iterations: exits early if DMA wrote a non-zero result to [bp+8]
lineComments.188F:7FA5: loop timed out or DMA completed — clean up DMA and restore IRQ5 vector
lineComments.188F:57F1: [0x38A6] = SB base port (0x0220); add 0x0C gives 0x022C (DSP write port)
lineComments.188F:57FA: poll write-buffer-ready: read 0x22C; bit 7 set means busy — spin until clear before writing

gaps.188F:13B3: SB hardware IRQ handler; passed as DX=0x13B3 to func_17F0 by func_start_audio for installation
